'''Jack Ormondroyd''' (born 7 November 1991) is a professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} for the [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[Betfred Super League]], on a season-long loan from [[Oldham Roughyeds|Oldham]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/jack-ormondroyds-salford-exit-confirmed-as-long-term-championship-deal-agreed-2|title=Jack Ormondroyd’s Salford exit confirmed as long-term Championship deal agreed|publisher=loverugbyleague|accessdate=7 August 2025}}</ref>

He played for [[Featherstone Rovers]] in two separate spells in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]]. Ormondroyd played for the [[Leeds Rhinos]] in the [[Super League]], and on loan from [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] at [[Featherstone Rovers|Featherstone]] in the Championship and the [[York City Knights]] in [[RFL League One|League 1]].

==Background==
Ormondroyd was born in [[Birmingham, England]]. He is the son of English former professional footballer [[Ian Ormondroyd]].<ref name="Much-changed Featherstone ready for Bradford Bulls test">{{cite web|url=http://www.yorkpress.co.uk/news/14258518.Much_changed_Featherstone_ready_for_Bradford_Bulls_test/|title=Much-changed Featherstone ready for Bradford Bulls test|publisher=York Press|date=2016-02-06|accessdate=2016-09-21}}</ref>

==Career==
Ormondroyd played in Australia for the [[Thirlmere-Tahmoor Roosters|Thirlmere Roosters]] in [[Group 6 Rugby League|Group 6]] [[Country Rugby League]].<ref name="Ormondroyd junior the 'big daddy' of the family at 17 stone">{{cite web|url=http://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/13363235.Ormondroyd_junior_the__big_daddy__of_the_family_at_17_stone/|title=Ormondroyd junior the 'big daddy' of the family at 17 stone|publisher=Telegraph & Argus|date=2015-07-01|accessdate=2016-09-21}}</ref>
===Featherstone Rovers===
Ormondroyd then played for [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].<ref name="Leeds Rhinos: Leeds set to complete signing of Ormondroyd">{{cite web|url=http://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/leeds-rhinos/leeds-rhinos-leeds-set-to-complete-signing-of-ormondroyd-1-8004526|title=Leeds Rhinos: Leeds set to complete signing of Ormondroyd|publisher=Yorkshire Evening Post|date=7 September 2016|accessdate=21 September 2016}}</ref>

Ormondroyd played seven games for Leeds in the 2017 Super League season but did not feature in the clubs playoffs campaign or their grand final victory over [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]]. 
In the 2019 RFL Championship season, Ormondroyd played 29 games for Featherstone including their [[Million Pound Game]] loss against [[Toronto Wolfpack|Toronto]]. In 2020, Ormondroyd joined Salford. In the 2022 Super League season, he played 26 games including the clubs semi-final loss against St Helens. In the 2023 Super League season, he played 24 matches for Salford as the club finished 7th on the table and missed the playoffs. In the 2024 Super League season, Ormondroyd managed only 13 appearances for the year with Salford.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.salfordnow.co.uk/2023/10/02/salford-red-devils-2023-season-in-review/|title=Salford Red Devils' 2023 season in review|website=www.salfordnow.co.uk|date=2 October 2023 }}</ref>

===Oldham RLFC===
On 7 August 2025 it was reported that he had signed for [[Oldham RLFC]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on a 2½-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=7 Aug 2025|author=George Riley|title=Jack Ormondroyd’s Salford exit confirmed as long-term Championship deal agreed|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/jack-ormondroyds-salford-exit-confirmed-as-long-term-championship-deal-agreed-2}}</ref>

===Bradford Bulls (loan)===
On 19 March 2026 it was reported that he had signed for [[Bradford Bulls]] on season-long loan<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=19 Mar 2026|author=Matthew Shaw|title=Bradford Bulls sign Super League veteran in fresh Salford Red Devils link|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/bradford-bulls-sign-super-league-veteran-in-fresh-salford-red-devils-link}}</ref>
